title: School enrollment, primary (% net)
definition: Net enrollment rate is the ratio of children of official school age who are enrolled in school to the population of the corresponding official school age. Primary education provides children with basic reading, writing, and mathematics skills along with an elementary understanding of such subjects as history, geography, natural science, social science, art, and music.

source.sourceOrganization: Stat Bulk Data Download Service, UN Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organization (UNESCO), uri: https://uis.unesco.org/bdds, publisher: UNESCO Institute for Statistics (UIS)

editorial.shortHook: A candidate map for net primary enrollment.
editorial.patternNote: The darkest countries include Norway, Canada, Iceland, while the lightest include Liberia, Eritrea, Djibouti; that contrast is the fastest way to read the net primary enrollment pattern.
editorial.whyItMatters: Net primary enrollment may add useful WORLDPRINT variety once source coverage, unit clarity, map interest, and distractor ambiguity are reviewed.

bestProbeCountries.reason: High anchor: this country sits near the darkest end of the map.
Low anchor: this country clarifies the lightest end of the scale.
Middle check: this country helps separate a true pattern from one-off outliers.

commonConfusions.reason: Out-of-school primary age lives in the same category, so it can feel plausible until the high and low countries are checked.
Secondary enrollment lives in the same category, so it can feel plausible until the high and low countries are checked.
Infant mortality overlaps with part of the visible pattern, but the full country spread does not match.

editorial.difficultyReason: Standard difficulty: the map has readable regional structure, but the best answer still depends on checking the unit and country anchors.
regionalSignals: Education indicators can be shaped by enrollment systems and data coverage.
Compare youth-heavy countries with higher-income service economies.
